title: Strict OOF regime-wise calibration and narrow stack
summary: The current plateau suggests ordering is already strong, so remaining leaderboard gain may come from probability shaping and model diversity.
rationale: The current plateau suggests ordering is already strong, so remaining leaderboard gain may come from probability shaping and model diversity. Beta calibration addresses skewed score distortions; Venn-Abers gives a strong calibration framework. A small stack over heterogeneous base models is more plausible than more same-family feature variants.
implementation_hint: Build OOF predictions from 2-3 diverse bases such as the current GBDT, CatBoost, and the ranker score. Fit tiny second-stage calibrators only on OOF predictions, separately for coarse regimes like dry vs wet and early/mid/late race; compare beta calibration, isotonic, or Venn-Abers-style maps. Keep race-blocked folds end-to-end.
expected_effect: Likely a modest but real public-score improvement if test distribution differs slightly from local CV and if the base models make complementary mistakes.
risk: Calibration can hurt AUC if regimes are too small or if the calibrator is fit on noisy folds. Any in-fold stacking or globally fit calibrator will leak and overstate gains.
